coverageGaps.reason: Required tool(s) not found on PATH: sonar-scanner
Required tool(s) not found on PATH: cover-agent
Required tool(s) not found on PATH: gitleaks

howItWorks: Detect the project ecosystem(s) and select the applicable scanners.
Preflight: verify the app has the inputs needed for a meaningful scan (POC→Enterprise readiness).
Run security/quality/test scanners AND per-component evaluators (fail-safe, robustness, coherence).
Deduplicate findings, compute the readiness score, and evaluate the Quality Gate.
Emit human reports (console / HTML / PDF) and a machine fix-manifest for coding agents (Claude Code).

bands.meaning: Caution — address before the next deploy.

model.formula: 100 − Σ ( weight × log₂(1 + count) ) per severity, clamped to 0–100
model.notes: Severity dominates volume (one CRITICAL outweighs many LOWs) and repeated findings of the same severity have diminishing impact. The score is a health indicator — the pass/fail decision is the Quality Gate, not the score.

thisReport.formula: 100 − Σ ( weight × log₂(1 + count) ) per severity, clamped to 0–100

CRITICAL.meaning: An exploitable flaw or breaking defect that puts production at immediate risk.

HIGH.meaning: A serious security or correctness issue likely to cause harm in production.
HIGH.action: Fix before release; blocks the gate by default.

MEDIUM.meaning: A real weakness that increases risk but is not immediately exploitable.
MEDIUM.action: Plan a fix soon; advisory by default.

LOW.meaning: A minor or cosmetic issue with limited impact.

security.label: Security
security.whatItMeans: A vulnerability an attacker could exploit.
security.whyItMatters: Directly exposes the application or its data to attack.

defect.label: Defect
defect.whatItMeans: The application behaves incorrectly (e.g. a failing test).
defect.whyItMatters: Broken logic reaches users and erodes trust.

robustness.label: Robustness
robustness.whatItMeans: Missing resilience: no timeout, no error handling, no input validation.
robustness.whyItMatters: The app fails unpredictably under load, bad input, or upstream outages.

coherence.label: Coherence
coherence.whatItMeans: Components disagree (e.g. a UI call that an endpoint will reject).
coherence.whyItMatters: Mismatches cause runtime failures that unit tests miss.

fail-safe.label: Fail-safe
fail-safe.whatItMeans: A control lacks a safe default or handled failure path.
fail-safe.whyItMatters: When something goes wrong, the app does the unsafe thing.

best-practice.label: Best practice
best-practice.whatItMeans: Deviation from a hardening or quality convention.
best-practice.whyItMatters: Accumulated drift makes the system harder to secure and maintain.

coverage.label: Coverage gap
coverage.whatItMeans: A check could not run (missing tool or input), so this area is unverified.
coverage.whyItMatters: An unverified area is not the same as a safe one.

glossary.gate: The Quality Gate is the deploy decision. It FAILS when the scan finds any issue at a severity listed in `failOn` (default: CRITICAL, HIGH), or when a scanner errored. Otherwise it PASSES. This is independent of the readiness score.

not-production-safe.title: ⛔ NOT PRODUCTION-SAFE
not-production-safe.meaning: Critical inputs or checks are missing — correctness/security is unverified.
not-production-safe.nextStep: Fix the CRITICAL items to reach Production-Safe.

production-safe.title: 🟡 PRODUCTION-SAFE
production-safe.meaning: The security baseline is met, but enterprise-grade hardening gaps remain.
production-safe.nextStep: Close the HIGHLY ADVISED gaps to reach Enterprise-Grade.

enterprise-grade.title: ✅ ENTERPRISE-GRADE
enterprise-grade.meaning: Required and recommended inputs are all configured and verified.
enterprise-grade.nextStep: Maintain coverage as the app evolves.

findings.title: rules.dat-no-process-exit
findings.rationale: Avoid using process.exit() in application code. It abruptly terminates the Node.js process. Throw an error instead.

findings.title: javascript.lang.security.detect-child-process.detect-child-process
findings.rationale: Detected calls to child_process from a function argument `command`. This could lead to a command injection if the input is user controllable. Try to avoid calls to child_process, and if it is needed ensure user input is correctly sanitized or sandboxed.

findings.title: rules.dat-no-process-exit
findings.rationale: Avoid using process.exit() in application code. It abruptly terminates the Node.js process. Throw an error instead.

findings.title: javascript.lang.security.audit.code-string-concat.code-string-concat
findings.rationale: Found data from an Express or Next web request flowing to `eval`. If this data is user-controllable this can lead to execution of arbitrary system commands in the context of your application process. Avoid `eval` whenever possible.

findings.title: rules.dat-no-console-log
findings.rationale: Avoid using console.log() in production code. Use a structured logging library like Winston or Pino.

findings.title: python.lang.security.audit.dynamic-urllib-use-detected.dynamic-urllib-use-detected
findings.rationale: Detected a dynamic value being used with urllib. urllib supports 'file://' schemes, so a dynamic value controlled by a malicious actor may allow them to read arbitrary files. Audit uses of urllib calls to ensure user data cannot control the URLs, or consider using the 'requests' library instead.

location.excerpt:

21:             f.write(response.read())

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.xss.direct-response-write.direct-response-write
findings.rationale: Detected directly writing to a Response object from user-defined input. This bypasses any HTML escaping and may expose your application to a Cross-Site-scripting (XSS) vulnerability. Instead, use 'resp.render()' to render safely escaped HTML.

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.xss.direct-response-write.direct-response-write
findings.rationale: Detected directly writing to a Response object from user-defined input. This bypasses any HTML escaping and may expose your application to a Cross-Site-scripting (XSS) vulnerability. Instead, use 'resp.render()' to render safely escaped HTML.

findings.title: LOW-TEST-COVERAGE
findings.rationale: Overall line coverage (33.33%) is below the required threshold (80%).

findings.title: CKV_AWS_260
findings.rationale: Ensure no security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0:0 to port 80

findings.title: CKV_AWS_24
findings.rationale: Ensure no security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0:0 to port 22

findings.title: COMP-ENDPOINT-NOAUTH
findings.rationale: State-changing endpoint POST /api/login has no authentication middleware — it can be invoked by anyone.
findings.suggestedFix: Require authentication before mutating state (e.g. add requireAuth/isAuthenticated middleware or verify the session/token in the handler).

findings.title: COMP-ENDPOINT-NOAUTH
findings.rationale: State-changing endpoint POST /api/transfer has no authentication middleware — it can be invoked by anyone.
findings.suggestedFix: Require authentication before mutating state (e.g. add requireAuth/isAuthenticated middleware or verify the session/token in the handler).

findings.title: DL3008
findings.rationale: Pin versions in apt get install. Instead of `apt-get install <package>` use `apt-get install <package>=<version>`

location.excerpt:
2: # Missing maintainer/user, using latest tag
3: 
4: # Installing packages without clean
5: RUN apt-get update && apt-get install python3
6: 
7: # Hardcoded password
8: ENV DATABASE_PASSWORD="super-secret-password-123"

findings.title: DL3014
findings.rationale: Use the `-y` switch to avoid manual input `apt-get -y install <package>`

location.excerpt:

findings.title: COMP-APICALL-NO-TIMEOUT
findings.rationale: API call GET /api/orders/42 has no timeout/abort — a hung upstream can freeze the request indefinitely (availability risk).
findings.suggestedFix: Pass an AbortController signal (or a timeout) so the call fails fast instead of hanging.

findings.title: COMP-APICALL-NO-ERROR-HANDLING
findings.rationale: State-changing API call POST url has no error handling — a failed request fails silently or crashes the UI.
findings.suggestedFix: Wrap the call in try/catch (or add .catch) and surface the failure to the user; never assume a mutating request succeeds.

findings.title: COMP-APICALL-NO-ERROR-HANDLING

findings.suggestedFix: Wrap the call in try/catch (or add .catch) and surface the failure to the user; never assume a mutating request succeeds.

findings.title: COMP-NET-OPEN-WORLD
findings.rationale: aws_security_group "allow_all" allows ingress from the entire internet (0.0.0.0/0) on port(s) 0.

location.excerpt:
20: }
21: 
22: # Add obvious misconfiguration: Security group open to the world
23: resource "aws_security_group" "allow_all" {
24:   name        = "allow_all"
25:   description = "Allow all inbound traffic"
26:

findings.suggestedFix: Scope ingress CIDRs to the minimum required ranges; reserve 0.0.0.0/0 for genuinely public ports (80/443) behind a WAF/LB.

findings.title: COMP-CROSSSTACK-AUTH-MISMATCH
findings.rationale: GET /api/orders/42 (src/components/builder.test.ts) calls GET /api/orders/:id, which requires authentication, but sends no Authorization header — this request will be rejected.
findings.suggestedFix: Attach the auth token (Authorization header) to the client call, or relax the endpoint if it is meant to be public.

findings.title: COMP-CROSSSTACK-AUTH-MISMATCH
findings.rationale: GET /api/profile (src/components/extractors/react.test.ts) calls GET /api/profile, which requires authentication, but sends no Authorization header — this request will be rejected.
findings.suggestedFix: Attach the auth token (Authorization header) to the client call, or relax the endpoint if it is meant to be public.
